title: Vista: How Soon Until Deployment?
author: David Garrett
abstract: NewsFactor reports analyst views on enterprise Vista adoption pace. Peter Kastner, Aberdeen vice-president and research director for enterprise technology, frames a three-year hardware-lifecycle 'cadence' that places full Vista deployment in 2010 as new Vista-laden machines replace XP. Kastner predicts Microsoft will stop shipping XP in small business retail systems in 2007, forcing Vista uptake. He disagrees with Gartner's recommendation that businesses wait until 2008.
importance_rationale: Captures enterprise Vista-adoption cadence prediction at the eve of Vista launch — central to understanding why Vista underperformed and why enterprises skipped to Windows 7 (2009-2012).
relevance_rationale: OS-rollout cadence framework remains relevant; specific Vista context historical.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's hardware-cadence model proved correct directionally — Vista was largely skipped by enterprises in favor of Windows 7 by 2010, validating the slow-uptake thesis but not the specific 'fully deployed by 2010' prediction.
